title: Eva unites us all
wikitext:
[]: Eva unites us all             Quote Post by IX » Sat Aug 09, 2008 1:43 am as an evamonkey and someone who was watching psych and stumbled feet first into the first 5 min of the 08 summer Olympics, and lost interest, how does the LONGEST sport-game-thing of the year unite people, yeah nations, but real people, not just a nation in general, not only sportsmen and diplomats, but average monkeys like you and me

[]:             Quote Post by IX » Sat Aug 09, 2008 1:45 am that and the fact that eva has gotten me together with people in many places of the world

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sat Aug 09, 2008 1:47 am Don't double post. As some of the Great People say, the edit button is your friend. Answer to your question: Olympics is more of a competition, and doesn't unite. Eva unites us, and it shall unite the world to bring it under the rule of Anime in general. This is, the rather extremist point of view, but that is how I am.

[]:             Quote Post by chazthesilencer » Sat Aug 09, 2008 5:09 am Eva is uniting all of us... not us all, unfortunately.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sat Aug 09, 2008 6:07 am If we think about the global scale, plus the ones who do not know of our spiffing existance but Eva fans nonetheless, it would add up to quite a large population, and that is a start, I guess.

[]: Re: Eva unites us all             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Aug 09, 2008 11:04 am Eva depresses me, and makes me want to shut everybody out of my world. IX wrote: evamonkey

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Sat Aug 09, 2008 12:34 pm It's the point of veiw you watch Eva from. If you watch it and try to be depressed, 99% of the time you will be. But if you try to watch it from an optemistic point of veiw, you may discover a thing or two about yourself. But I guess the fact that all nations getting together to do something is the only way to get everyone involved except for war. I guess the fact that everyone wants to win classifes us as 'uniting' together. With Eva we actually have something in common and can relate to each other about.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Sat Aug 09, 2008 12:50 pm If you ask me, I don't like the idea of Eva "uniting" everyone. It reminds me too much of the HIP, and I never liked that. SO if a movie about joining all souls as one in the end managed to unite the whole world, that would be frak'd up.

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Sat Aug 09, 2008 12:55 pm I second what Action Bastard said. That would be freaky . 'Sides, the show has so many interpretations, quite personal at that, that I don't think it is even true. It sets us apart, but in a good way. It teaches us about our individual selves. Plus, I find it weird to read the tagline "much better than the Olympics" when I have the Olympics running on a TV in the background.

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Sat Aug 09, 2008 1:01 pm But that's the best thing about Eva. There are so many interpretations, so we can share with each other what it means to us and reflect on each other.

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Sat Aug 09, 2008 1:36 pm True. But not everyones interpretations are going to be of much use to other people. If you can get someone to accept your own interpretation, more power to you. But the variety of interpretations means you will inevitably find something of meaning to you. Even if that something is not what other people are getting out of it.

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Sat Aug 09, 2008 1:44 pm Carl Horn once told me: "Anime and Manga unite us all. Much better than the Olympics." I guess it's true.

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Sat Aug 09, 2008 2:03 pm The main problem with the Olympics is the inherent politics of the thing. Nations try to show each other up in a field besides the battlefield. You are expected to root for your own country (I never do). And occasionally it causes political incidents which are no good for anyone. The main problem with anime is the inherent politics of the thing. Except its a matter of accepting an art form from another country, something a lot of Americans have a problem doing. (when was the last time you saw a European film? Or any other non-anime foreign media?) Add in the animation ghetto that plagues most Americans in a way that doesn't seem to be a problem elsewhere, and most Americans seem to think that its for freaks. (sadly, some otaku think this is a good thing ) So they are different, but it comes to a similar effect.

[]:             Quote Post by Barinax » Sat Aug 09, 2008 2:52 pm I don't feel united with anyone due to Eva, or anime for that matter. To be honest, I never liked calling myself a fan of anime, or anything for that matter. I don't know anybody in real life that I know likes Eva, although I have shared some interests in anime. Well, I'm not very open myself, so I guess if anyone is like me, I won't find that person and that person won't find me. On the Internet, I feel alienated from all of you. I don't know. I don't go to many communities on the Internet. Wait... what? Disregard that,

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Sat Aug 09, 2008 3:29 pm I'm not a fan of anime, I like Evangelion and then maybe 2 others. That's it. And this is the only community I am active inon the internet. But you people are my second family, I can turn here for advice if I need it or any problems I have.

[]:             Quote Post by Barinax » Sat Aug 09, 2008 3:46 pm I do like some anime (much more than two) but I don't like saying I do for multiple reason. Does that make sense?

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Sat Aug 09, 2008 3:50 pm A lot of anime in my opinion is crap and I can see why you would say that you don't like anime. I don't say I like anime, I say I like Evangelion, and that's it.

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Sat Aug 09, 2008 5:31 pm Anime is just a medium for telling stories. If the stories are good, then i like that anime. It has some advantages and disadvantages that influences the kind of stories that get told, but for me that means that they tend towards speculative fiction, which is my real forte.

[]:             Quote Post by Barinax » Sat Aug 09, 2008 6:14 pm I won't say it, but if someone asks me if I like anime, I might say 'some'. But not yes. I don't like to generalize that much in real life, so I might start mentioning specifics. I suppose I like series where the characters are people I can relate to, people that can see real. (This doesn't mean that I can't enjoy anything else, but these tend to be my favorites.) Recently I discovered that I found it hard to imagine myself... or how I look, but I can easily imagine Shinji in my place. Well, I can imagine anyone well except for myself.

[]: Re: Eva unites us all             Quote Post by IX » Sat Aug 09, 2008 9:52 pm Sachi_13 wrote: Eva depresses me, and makes me want to shut everybody out of my world. IX wrote: evamonkey
